Loncophylla sulphurea, commonly known as Loncoxis sulphurea, is a plant species that belongs to the family Rubiaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The bioactive constituents of Loncophylla sulphurea include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Loncophylla sulphurea is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever and respiratory issues.
